justification: All pass criteria met: Only v2 content used (AC1+AC3, no v1 AC2); v2 domain rule uses absolute prohibition language ('cannot be disabled under any circumstances — this is a legal requirement'); AC3 present with exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; contradictions_resolved section explicitly notes v2 supersedes v1 post-legal review; MJ's 403 enforcement aligned with v2 prohibition throughout architecture_guidance.

observations.text: Contradictions_resolved section clearly documents that Bird v1 AC2 is void and v2 is authoritative due to legal review
Exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED appears in domain_rules, architecture_guidance, and acceptance_criteria consistently
No trace of v1 AC2 (transactional email opt-out) in acceptance criteria — only v2 content present
Domain rule uses v2's absolute prohibition language, not v1's softer 'cannot be fully disabled'
Architecture guidance explicitly specifies 403 (not 400/422) matching Bird v2 and MJ alignment
Bonus: identifies a genuine open question about the true-value no-op edge case that neither source addressed
Fail-closed behavior on mixed requests (AC3) demonstrates deep understanding of the legal constraint's implications
